options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -abstract_blockage_cut_around_pin
options.description: For std cell, we do not want cover obs on pin. So: If -input_cell_type {std}, setting the value to all metal and via layer name list. If -input_cell_type {block} or -input_cell_type {io}, setting the value to all via layer name list.

options.name: -antenna_connectivity
options.description: Specifies the layers for which connectivity is to be extracted. The syntax is {<layer1> <connect_layer> <layer2>}.

options.name: -antenna_diffusion_geom
options.description: Specifies diffusion layer, followed by expression to remove gate channel area from diffusion region.

options.name: -antenna_gate_geom
options.description: Specifies poly layer that forms a gate, and then the layer expression to derive the gate area. This option also triggers the creation of antenna models.

options.name: -blockage_detailed_layers
options.description: Specifies the layers for which a detailed blockage model is to be created. The detailed blockage model generates blockages only where there are real shapes in the cell on the layer. This only applies to IO cells and block cells, it does not apply to standard cells.

options.name: -boundary_layers
options.description: By default this list is empty, and the prBoundary is used. The prBoundary comes from an explicit GDS layer that is used to compute the LEF SIZE (and OVERLAP OBS shapes for rectilinear boundaries). If there is no explicit GDS prBoundary layer, the boundary can be computed from the bounding box of geometries on this list of layers. Note, this option is almost never correct for core standard cells.

options.name: -cell_symmetry
options.description: Specifies a property value for cell symmetry. The valid values for the property symmetry include: {R0 | X | Y | R90 | X Y | X R90 | Y R90 | X Y R90}. Default {X Y}

options.name: -export_lef_version
options.description: Version of LEF to export. The default version is the same as the version of technology LEF file being imported.

options.name: -extract_layers_power
options.description: Specifies the layers through which the extractor extracts each power net. This is used to reduce the search space if abstract is taking too much run time for each cell. Usually not applicable to standard cells. For blocks it is common to only extract the power nets on the top one or two metal layers.

options.name: -extract_layers_signal
options.description: Specifies the layers through which the extractor extracts each net. This is used to reduce the search space if abstract is taking too much run time for each cell. Usually not applicable to standard cells.

options.name: -extract_pin_layers_power
options.description: Determines which of the shapes found by the extractor should be turned into pins. This argument is valid only if the associated layer, geometry specification and connectivity have been provided in the technology LEF. Usually not applicable to standard cells.

options.name: -extract_pin_layers_signal
options.description: Determines which of the shapes found by the extractor should be turned into pins. This argument is valid only if the associated layer, geometry specification and connectivity have been provided in the technology LEF. Usually not applicable to standard cells.

options.name: -input_cell_type
options.description: Choose the cell type being translated with abstract. The tool adjusts its settings based on this selection. Optional.

options.name: -input_gds_layer_map_file
options.description: Specify the file that maps GDS layer number / data type to LEF layer names in the Virtuoso format. Required. For example, the gds file is not colorized, you could use the common virtuoso format gds layer map to stream gds file. #layer name #purpose name #stream layer #stream data type V0 drawing 35 0 V0 drawing 35 235 V0 drawing 35 236 M1 drawing 15 0 M1 drawing 15 235 M1 drawing 15 236 M1 drawing 15 239 For example, the gds file is colorized, you could specify the mask color in gds layer map to stream gds file. #layer name #purpose name #stream layer #stream data type #photo mask color #color state V0 drawing 35 0 V0 drawing 35 235 mask1Color unlocked V0 drawing 35 236 mask2Color unlocked M1 drawing 15 0 M1 drawing 15 235 mask1Color unlocked M1 drawing 15 236 mask2Color unlocked M1 drawing 15 239 mask3Color unlocked

options.name: -input_lef_tech_file
options.description: Input technology LEF file. This is used to get all the layer names, their types (routing, cut, etc.) and the layer order for extracting connectivity. Required.

options.name: -keep_temp_files
options.description: Do not clean up temporary files/libraries that are created in the process after command finishes.

options.name: -pins_analog_names
options.description: Identifies analog pin names based on label strings in the Abstract to set the LEF "USE ANALOG" property. Enter a list of possible names for the analog pins. This can be a list of regular expressions, each separated by a space.

options.name: -pins_clock_names
options.description: Identifies clock pin names based on label strings in the Abstract to set the LEF "USE CLOCK" property. Enter a list of possible names for the clock pins. This can be a list of regular expressions, each separated by a space.

options.name: -pins_ground_names
options.description: Identifies ground pins based on label strings in the Abstract to set the LEF "USE GROUND" property. Enter a list of possible names for the ground pins. This can be a list of regular expressions, each separated by a space.

options.name: -pins_output_names
options.description: Identifies output pins based on label strings in the GDS to set the LEF direction to OUTPUT. Enter a list of regular expressions separated by spaces.

options.name: -pins_power_names
options.description: Identifies power pin names based on label strings in the Abstract to set the LEF "USE POWER" property. Enter a list of possible names for the power pins. This can be a list of regular expressions, each separated by a space.

options.name: -pins_text_pin_map
options.description: Maps text labels to pins on nets. Specify the layer purpose pairs based on which you want the abstract generator to search for geometry of any given layer-purpose pair with a text label.

options.name: -pre_skill
options.description: a skill file that will be executed in abstract before the auto-generated script is executed. Optional.

options.name: -selected_cells
options.description: Select cells to create abstract for using regex style expressions. Optional.

options.name: -site_name
options.description: Site to be used for macros. This site will be added to all the cells.

options.name: -verbose
options.description: Verbose mode with extra print.

options.name: -quiet
options.description: Displays the current settings in Tcl list format only

options.name: -nonDefault
options.description: Displays the current settings which are not default settings
